# TinyUa

> A lightweight OPC UA client stack for .NET 8, implemented from scratch. Core, Transport, and
> Client have no dependency on the OPC Foundation SDK.

## Overview

TinyUa implements OPC UA binary encoding, UA TCP framing/chunking, secure channels, sessions,
Read/Write/Browse services, subscriptions, and reconnect recovery. Cryptography uses .NET 8
`System.Security.Cryptography`. The OPC Foundation SDK is used only by `TinyUa.Example` and
`TinyUa.Security.Tests` to host an in-process reference server.

Public client namespaces:

- `TinyUa.Client`
- `TinyUa.Client.Services`
- `TinyUa.Client.Subscriptions`
- `TinyUa.Client.Discovery`
- `TinyUa.Core.Types`
- `TinyUa.Core.Security`
- `TinyUa.Core.Logging`

Do not use the removed `TinyUa.Core.Client*` namespaces.

## Build and Self-Test

```bash
dotnet build TinyUa.sln
dotnet test TinyUa.Client.Tests/TinyUa.Client.Tests.csproj
dotnet test TinyUa.Security.Tests/TinyUa.Security.Tests.csproj
dotnet run --project TinyUa.Example -- selftest
```

## Quick Start

```csharp
using TinyUa.Client;

await using var client = await UaClient
    .ConnectTo("opc.tcp://server:4840")
    .WithAppName("MyApp")
    .WithSecurity("Basic256Sha256")
    .WithUserName("user", "pass")
    .BuildAndRunAsync();

var time = await client.ReadValueAsync<DateTime>("i=2258");
Console.WriteLine($"ServerTime: {time:HH:mm:ss.fff}");

var result = await client.WriteAsync("ns=2;s=SetPoint", 42.0);
Console.WriteLine(result?.StatusCode.IsGood == true
    ? "OK"
    : result?.StatusCode.GetStatusText() ?? "no result");
```

## Connection and Configuration

Fluent builder methods:

- `.WithSecurity("Basic256Sha256")` — `None`, `Basic256Sha256`,
  `Aes128Sha256RsaOaep`, or `Aes256Sha256RsaPss`
- `.WithSecurity(options => ...)` — configure mode, identity, and certificates
- `.WithUserName(user, pass)` — username/password authentication
- `.WithAppName("MyApp")`
- `.WithRequestTimeout(5000)` — per-step network timeout; default 30000 ms
- `.WithSessionTimeout(ms)`
- `.WithReconnectRetries(max)` — default -1 means unlimited
- `.WithoutReconnect()` — equivalent to zero reconnect attempts
- `.WithErrorMode(ErrorMode.ReturnNull)`
- `.WithSubscriptionDispatch(options => ...)`
- `.EnableLog(...)`, `.EnableLogFile(...)`, `.WithLogger(...)`
- `.Build()` — create without connecting
- `.BuildAndRunAsync()` — create and connect

Important `UaClientOptions` properties:

- `Timeout`
- `SessionTimeout`, `SessionKeepAliveIntervalMs`, `ChannelLifetime`
- `WarmupOnConnect`, `MaxMessageSize`
- `ReconnectMaxRetries`, `ReconnectInitialDelayMs`, `ReconnectMaxDelayMs`
- `MaxPublishRequests`
- `SubscriptionDispatch.QueueCapacity`, `SubscriptionDispatch.OverflowPolicy`
- `ErrorMode`
- `Security.Policy`, `Security.Mode`
- `Security.AutoDiscoverServerCertificate`, `Security.AutoAcceptServerCertificate`
- `Security.UserIdentity`
- `Security.Certificate`

`UaClient` deep-clones options during construction. Later mutations to the caller's options do not
affect the running client.

## Read

```csharp
using TinyUa.Core.Types;

var single = await client.ReadAsync("i=2258");
if (single is { StatusCode.IsGood: true })
    Console.WriteLine($"{single.NodeId}: {single.DataValue?.Value?.Value}");

var results = await client.ReadAsync(new NodeId[] { "i=2256", "i=2258" })
    ?? throw new InvalidOperationException("Read returned no results.");
foreach (var result in results)
{
    Console.WriteLine(result.StatusCode.IsGood
        ? $"{result.NodeId}: {result.DataValue?.Value?.Value}"
        : $"{result.NodeId}: {result.StatusCode.GetStatusText()}");
}

var time = await client.ReadValueAsync<DateTime>("i=2258");
```

- Single Read returns `ReadResult?`; batch Read returns `ReadResult[]?`.
- `ReadResult` contains non-nullable `NodeId` and `StatusCode`, plus nullable `DataValue`.
- Check `result.StatusCode`, not `result.DataValue?.StatusCode`.
- `ReadValueAsync<T>` performs an exact CLR cast and returns `default(T)` on null/type mismatch; it
  does not call `Convert.ChangeType`.

## Write

```csharp
using TinyUa.Client.Services;
using TinyUa.Core.Types;

var single = await client.WriteAsync("ns=2;s=SetPoint", 42.0);
Console.WriteLine(single?.StatusCode.GetStatusText() ?? "no result");

var results = await client.WriteAsync(new WriteValue[]
{
    new() { NodeId = "ns=2;s=Scalar.Double", Value = new DataValue(3.14) },
    new() { NodeId = "ns=2;s=Scalar.Float",  Value = new DataValue(2.718f) },
    new() { NodeId = "ns=2;s=Scalar.Int32",  Value = new DataValue(42) }
}) ?? throw new InvalidOperationException("Write returned no results.");

foreach (var result in results)
    Console.WriteLine($"{result.NodeId}: {result.StatusCode.GetStatusText()}");
```

- Single Write returns `WriteResult?`; batch Write returns `WriteResult[]?`.
- `WriteResult` contains non-nullable `NodeId` and `StatusCode`.
- `DataValue(object)` and `new Variant(value)` infer the OPC UA type internally.
- An explicit `VariantType` converts scalar values through the constructor's internal conversion
  path, for example `double` to `VariantType.Float`.

## Browse

```csharp
var results = await client.BrowseAsync("i=85")
    ?? throw new InvalidOperationException("Browse returned no results.");
foreach (var reference in results[0].References ?? [])
    Console.WriteLine($"{reference.DisplayName?.Text} ({reference.NodeClass}): {reference.NodeId}");

var continuationPoint = results[0].ContinuationPoint;
if (continuationPoint is { Length: > 0 })
{
    var next = await client.BrowseNextAsync(continuationPoint)
        ?? throw new InvalidOperationException("BrowseNext returned no results.");
    foreach (var reference in next[0].References ?? [])
        Console.WriteLine($"{reference.DisplayName?.Text}: {reference.NodeId}");
}
```

## Subscriptions and Backpressure

```csharp
using TinyUa.Client;
using TinyUa.Client.Subscriptions;

await using var client = await UaClient
    .ConnectTo("opc.tcp://server:4840")
    .WithSubscriptionDispatch(options =>
    {
        options.QueueCapacity = 64;
        options.OverflowPolicy = NotificationOverflowPolicy.Wait;
    })
    .BuildAndRunAsync();

var subscription = await client.SubscribeAsync<double>(
    "ns=2;s=Temperature",
    (value, status) =>
    {
        if (status.IsGood)
            Console.WriteLine(value);
    },
    interval: 500);

Console.WriteLine($"pending={subscription.PendingNotificationMessages}");
Console.WriteLine($"dropped={subscription.DroppedNotificationMessages}");
subscription.Dispose();
```

- `DataChangeHandler`: `(NodeId nodeId, object? value, StatusCode status)`.
- `DataChangeHandlerEx` adds nullable source and server timestamps.
- One session-level `PublishEngine` maintains the finite `MaxPublishRequests` credit window.
- Each subscription has one bounded, ordered callback queue and one worker.
- `Wait` is the lossless default and propagates backpressure to the Publish window.
- `DropOldest` favors recent data; `DropNewest` preserves the queued backlog.
- Different subscriptions have independent callback workers.
- Subscriptions are Republished or rebuilt during reconnect.

## Reconnect and State Events

```csharp
using TinyUa.Client;

await using var client = UaClient
    .ConnectTo("opc.tcp://server:4840")
    .WithReconnectRetries(-1)
    .Build();

client.StateChanged += state => Console.WriteLine($"state={state}");
client.ReconnectBackoff += (attempt, delayMs) =>
    Console.WriteLine($"attempt={attempt}, delay={delayMs} ms");
client.SubscriptionsRecovered += lossless =>
    Console.WriteLine($"subscriptions lossless={lossless}");

await client.RunAsync();
```

`UaClient` is the single owner of `ClientState`. Socket and request failure signals enter through
one idempotent reconnect path. `ReconnectEngine` coalesces concurrent callers onto one reconnect
task. `StateChanged(Reconnecting)` is therefore emitted once per actual transition, not once per
failure signal.

## StatusCode

```csharp
status.IsGood;
status.IsBad;
status.IsUncertain;
status.GetStatusText();
```

`GetStatusText()` returns names such as `Good`, `BadNodeIdUnknown`, `BadNotWritable`, or a
hexadecimal fallback for unknown codes.

## Endpoint Discovery

```csharp
using TinyUa.Client.Discovery;

var endpoints = await EndpointDiscoverer.DiscoverAsync("opc.tcp://server:4840");
foreach (var endpoint in endpoints)
    Console.WriteLine($"{endpoint.SecurityPolicy} / {endpoint.SecurityMode}: {endpoint.EndpointUrl}");
```

`DiscoveredEndpoint` exposes `EndpointUrl`, `SecurityPolicy`, `SecurityPolicyUri`, `SecurityMode`,
`SecurityLevel`, `UserTokenTypes`, `IsSecure`, and `DisplayText`.

## Security

| Policy | RSA Encryption | Symmetric Crypto | Asymmetric Signature |
|--------|----------------|------------------|------------------------|
| None | — | — | — |
| Basic256Sha256 | RSA-OAEP-SHA1 | AES-256-CBC + HMAC-SHA256 | RSA-SHA256 |
| Aes128Sha256RsaOaep | RSA-OAEP-SHA1 | AES-128-CBC + HMAC-SHA256 | RSA-SHA256 |
| Aes256Sha256RsaPss | RSA-OAEP-SHA256 | AES-256-CBC + HMAC-SHA256 | RSA-PSS-SHA256 |

User tokens: Anonymous, UserName, X509 Certificate.

Secure endpoints normally discover the server certificate through GetEndpoints.
`AutoAcceptServerCertificate=false` enables built-in date, EKU, and chain-integrity checks.
Unknown self-signed roots remain allowed and CRL revocation is not checked.

## Buffer and Transport Design

- `BufferLease` is a class owner around `ArrayPool<byte>` to prevent copy/double-return bugs.
- Sensitive used bytes are zeroed before a pooled buffer is returned.
- Buffers larger than 1 MiB bypass pooling to avoid retaining one-off huge messages.
- `BinaryEncoderPool` is bounded and drops oversized encoders.
- The receiver uses one pooled ring buffer, drains multiple complete frames, and shrinks after
  oversized traffic.
- None-security messages can use gather send; secure/chunked messages stream pooled chunks.
- The `memory` benchmark exposes TinyUa rental/return, active/peak bytes, and encoder reuse. The
  private cache and hit rate inside `ArrayPool.Shared` are not observable.

## Live Verification

Live commands default to `opc.tcp://localhost:49320` and accept another URL after the subcommand:

```bash
dotnet run --project TinyUa.Benchmarks -- none
dotnet run --project TinyUa.Benchmarks -- substress
dotnet run --project TinyUa.Benchmarks -- memory
dotnet run --project TinyUa.Benchmarks -- basic
dotnet run --project TinyUa.Benchmarks -- security
```

`memory` runs until Ctrl+C and repeatedly batch-reads verified-writable variables below
`Objects/DemoCH/Data`, then writes the just-read values back while reporting allocation, GC, and
pool usage.

## Project Structure

| Project | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| TinyUa.Core | Binary encoding, OPC UA types, security, logging, pools |
| TinyUa.Transport | Message chunking, secure channel, token rotation |
| TinyUa.Client | Public client API, services, subscriptions, reconnect |
| TinyUa.Example | Console self-test with in-process OPCF server |
| TinyUa.Explorer | WPF browser and security endpoint selector |
| TinyUa.CertGen | WPF client-certificate generator |
| TinyUa.Benchmarks | Latency, correctness, subscription, memory/GC, crypto benchmarks |
| TinyUa.Client.Tests | Client concurrency and robustness tests |
| TinyUa.Security.Tests | Security integration tests with in-process OPCF server |
